Title: Fatty acyl-CoA reductase influences wax biosynthesis in the cotton mealybug, Tinsley

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: waxy layer / waxy filaments on the body surface (epicuticular surface, integument)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: reduces water loss and protects against insecticide permeation/desiccation stress
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: conversion of long-chain fatty acyl-CoAs to fatty alcohol intermediates in CHC biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: FAR family; N-terminal Rossmann fold NAD(P)+-binding domain; sterile alpha motif protein domain; cofactor-binding motif TGXXGF; active-site motif YXXXK
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: reduces fatty acyl-CoA to the corresponding fatty alcohol in the CHC biosynthetic pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies intermediate/precursor pool for CHC production and wax output
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: supported as a key control point for CHC/wax production in this species; formal rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: highest in third-instar nymphs and adult females; lowest in adult males; significantly highest in fat body of adult females
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: highest in third-instar nymphs and adult females; enriched in fat body
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: RNAi by dsRNA microinjection; oral RNAi via transgenic tobacco expressing dsPsFAR; low humidity (<10% RH); deltamethrin treatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: multi-omics screening (integument transcriptomics and proteomics); RACE cloning; RT-qPCR expression profiling; dsRNA microinjection RNAi; host-induced RNAi via transgenic tobacco; GC-MS metabolite profiling of CHCs; SEM; desiccation assay; insecticide survival assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes CHC biosynthesis and wax output; knockdown reduces total n-alkanes/CHCs and waxy layer, lowers desiccation tolerance, and increases susceptibility to deltamethrin
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsFAR (PSOL02039) was identified by integument transcriptome/proteome analyses as the FAR highly expressed in female nymphs and adults. 2. PsFAR expression is highest in fat body, third-instar nymphs, and adult females. 3. RNAi by microinjection or transgenic tobacco feeding significantly reduced PsFAR transcript levels and decreased CHC contents and wax secretion. 4. PsFAR knockdown increased mortality under desiccation (<10% RH) and after deltamethrin treatment, indicating wax-mediated protection.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: potential RNAi-based pest control target; host-induced gene silencing in transgenic plants could weaken wax barrier, increasing mealybug sensitivity to desiccation and insecticides
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: for pest-control deployment, suppressing PsFAR causes insect mortality and abnormal molting; no crop breeding value assessed
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: GenBank PRJNA798788; proteomics: PeptideAtlas PASS01729
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PsFAR mainly affects CHCs in waxy filaments rather than envelope integrity, as TEM showed no obvious envelope change after knockdown; same CHC classes remained but amounts of several n-alkanes fell; other FAR paralogs (e.g., PSOL06156, PsFAR I, PsFAR II) were not functionally validated as core genes in this study.
